title: betulaceae
intro: The family rank Betulaceae is a group of flowering plants that includes deciduous trees and shrubs commonly known as birches and alders. It is a distinct and cohesive group within the plant kingdom, characterized by a shared set of morphological and molecular traits. Members of the Betulaceae family are typically characterized by their simple, alternate leaves and catkin-like inflorescences, and are often found in temperate and subarctic regions. With approximately 160 species, the Betulaceae family encompasses a diverse range of species that have adapted to various environments and ecological niches.
